verses.text: The kingdom is like a king's marriage feast for his son, but the bidden would not come. Grace refused. Many slight the gracious invitation of God.
The kingdom is like a king's marriage feast for his son, but the bidden would not come. Grace refused. Many slight the gracious invitation of God.
The kingdom is like a king's marriage feast for his son, but the bidden would not come. Grace refused. Many slight the gracious invitation of God.
He sends again, but they make light of it and even slay his servants. Contempt and cruelty. The careless and the hostile alike spurn the call.
He sends again, but they make light of it and even slay his servants. Contempt and cruelty. The careless and the hostile alike spurn the call.
He sends again, but they make light of it and even slay his servants. Contempt and cruelty. The careless and the hostile alike spurn the call.
The king destroys the murderers and gathers all he finds, good and bad, to fill the feast. Open invitation. When the favoured refuse, grace goes out to all.
The king destroys the murderers and gathers all he finds, good and bad, to fill the feast. Open invitation. When the favoured refuse, grace goes out to all.
The king destroys the murderers and gathers all he finds, good and bad, to fill the feast. Open invitation. When the favoured refuse, grace goes out to all.
The king destroys the murderers and gathers all he finds, good and bad, to fill the feast. Open invitation. When the favoured refuse, grace goes out to all.
A man without a wedding garment is bound and cast out; 'many are called, but few are chosen.' Fitting righteousness. None may come to the feast in his own filthy rags.
A man without a wedding garment is bound and cast out; 'many are called, but few are chosen.' Fitting righteousness. None may come to the feast in his own filthy rags.
A man without a wedding garment is bound and cast out; 'many are called, but few are chosen.' Fitting righteousness. None may come to the feast in his own filthy rags.
A man without a wedding garment is bound and cast out; 'many are called, but few are chosen.' Fitting righteousness. None may come to the feast in his own filthy rags.
The Pharisees try to entangle Him: 'Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar?' A baited trap. They hope to catch Him whichever way He answers.
The Pharisees try to entangle Him: 'Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar?' A baited trap. They hope to catch Him whichever way He answers.
The Pharisees try to entangle Him: 'Is it lawful to give tribute unto Caesar?' A baited trap. They hope to catch Him whichever way He answers.
Whose image is on the coin? 'Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.' Both duties. We owe the state its due and God His.
Whose image is on the coin? 'Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.' Both duties. We owe the state its due and God His.
Whose image is on the coin? 'Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.' Both duties. We owe the state its due and God His.
Whose image is on the coin? 'Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.' Both duties. We owe the state its due and God His.
Whose image is on the coin? 'Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.' Both duties. We owe the state its due and God His.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They use a tale to ridicule the life to come.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They use a tale to ridicule the life to come.
The Sadducees, denying the resurrection, pose a woman with seven husbands. Mocking riddle. They use a tale to ridicule the life to come.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive of a world beyond marriage and death.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive of a world beyond marriage and death.
Whose wife shall she be in the resurrection? A snare in story form. They cannot conceive of a world beyond marriage and death.
'Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures'; in the resurrection they are 'as the angels.' Risen life. The world to come is greater and other than this one.
'Ye do err, not knowing the scriptures'; in the resurrection they are 'as the angels.' Risen life. The world to come is greater and other than this one.
God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — 'not the God of the dead, but of the living.' Proof of life. The patriarchs live still, for God is theirs.
God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — 'not the God of the dead, but of the living.' Proof of life. The patriarchs live still, for God is theirs.
God is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — 'not the God of the dead, but of the living.' Proof of life. The patriarchs live still, for God is theirs.
A lawyer asks which is the great commandment. The heart of duty. He seeks the sum of all God requires.
A lawyer asks which is the great commandment. The heart of duty. He seeks the sum of all God requires.
A lawyer asks which is the great commandment. The heart of duty. He seeks the sum of all God requires.
'Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart'; and 'thy neighbour as thyself'; 'on these... hang all the law and the prophets.' Love is all. The whole law is fulfilled in love to God and man.
'Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart'; and 'thy neighbour as thyself'; 'on these... hang all the law and the prophets.' Love is all. The whole law is fulfilled in love to God and man.
'Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart'; and 'thy neighbour as thyself'; 'on these... hang all the law and the prophets.' Love is all. The whole law is fulfilled in love to God and man.
'Love the Lord thy God with all thy heart'; and 'thy neighbour as thyself'; 'on these... hang all the law and the prophets.' Love is all. The whole law is fulfilled in love to God and man.
He asks the Pharisees, whose son is Christ? David's, they say; yet David calls Him Lord. A deeper truth. Messiah is not only David's son but his Lord.
He asks the Pharisees, whose son is Christ? David's, they say; yet David calls Him Lord. A deeper truth. Messiah is not only David's son but his Lord.
He asks the Pharisees, whose son is Christ? David's, they say; yet David calls Him Lord. A deeper truth. Messiah is not only David's son but his Lord.
If David in the Spirit calls Him Lord, how is He David's son? None could answer Him a word. Mystery of the God-man. Christ is both David's descendant and his divine Lord.
